nodes.name: PulseFullView.swift
nodes.summary: Full Pulse history screen (stub) for the Me|Us tab — owns window selection and camera state, hosts a scrollable PulseGraph plus an insights placeholder and a full-screen dot burn summary overlay.
nodes.languageNotes: SwiftUI view; geometry resolved via AppLayout.from(geo), no UIScreen usage.

nodes.name: PulseFullView
nodes.summary: View struct rendering the full Pulse timeline with window selector, horizontally/vertically scrollable graph, insights scaffold, and a dot-tap burn summary overlay.

nodes.name: PulseGraph.swift
nodes.summary: Core drawing primitive for the Pulse capacity timeline — a breathing spectrum EKG line with tier guide lines, tappable tier badges, animated welding sparks on the live segment, and an empty-state heartbeat demo.
nodes.languageNotes: Custom GraphicsContext Canvas drawing plus an Animatable subview (PulseGraphCanvas) driving drawProgress; reduce-motion fallbacks on every looping .task animation.

nodes.name: PulseGraph
nodes.summary: Public view wrapper that chooses a touch-glow button path or a plain canvas, runs the breath/heartbeat/demo animation tasks, and presents the TierGuideSheet when a tier badge is tapped.

nodes.name: PulseGraphCanvas
nodes.summary: Animatable canvas that computes graph geometry and renders tier guides, glow/crisp lines, live dot, dot overlays, and the welding-spark draw-in effect for the latest segment.

nodes.name: PulseSheetView.swift
nodes.summary: Sheet presented from the Pulse home widget — mirrors the widget header, shows streak/avg-tier/trend stats, a window selector and graph card, and a single 'Open in Map' exit.

nodes.name: PulseSheetView
nodes.summary: View struct computing streak, average tier and a trend label from entries, rendering the stats row, window buttons, an iridescent graph card and the Open-in-Map CTA.

nodes.name: PulseWidget.swift
nodes.summary: Compact Pulse timeline widget for the Home dashboard — reads entries from PulseStore, renders a chrome-free graph with a floating tier header, and launches the check-in cover and full-history sheet.

nodes.name: PulseWidget
nodes.summary: View struct binding to the injected PulseStore, presenting the PulseSheetView and CheckInShell, and committing a completed check-in entry back to the store.

nodes.name: PulseStore.swift
nodes.summary: Observable store owning the local list of daily PulseEntry check-ins, persisting them to UserDefaults with one-entry-per-day dedup and DEBUG preview seeding.

nodes.name: PulseStore
nodes.summary: @Observable store that loads, adds (deduping same-day), removes, and saves PulseEntry records to UserDefaults under a versioned key.

nodes.name: TierGuideSheet.swift
nodes.summary: Explanatory sheet presented when a Pulse tier badge is tapped, describing the four capacity tiers — Expansive, Sovereign, Protective, Contracted.

nodes.name: TierGuideSheet
nodes.summary: View struct listing the four capacity tiers with colored letter badges, names, sublabels and descriptions in a scrollable layout.

nodes.name: PresenceDebugView.swift
nodes.summary: DEBUG-only two-device harness (store + view) that joins a Supabase couple channel via RealtimeSessionService and tracks presence join/leave deltas; throwaway scaffold ahead of the real AirlockStore.
nodes.languageNotes: Wrapped in #if DEBUG; @Observable @MainActor store consumes Supabase RealtimeChannelV2 presence streams.

nodes.name: PresenceDebugStore
nodes.summary: @Observable @MainActor store that validates UUIDs, joins/leaves the realtime session channel, and maintains the present-user set from presence change deltas.

nodes.name: PresenceDebugView
nodes.summary: Debug view with couple/user ID fields, Join/Leave buttons, a connection status row, and a live present-user list.

nodes.name: SessionPlan.swift
nodes.summary: SwiftData @Model for a curated couple session plan — ordered card ids with optional per-card and global timers, preset/LDR flags, and a hardcoded stub for early two-device verification.
nodes.languageNotes: @Model persistable; live two-device state lives server-side in Supabase, this is local truth only.

nodes.name: SessionPlan
nodes.summary: SwiftData model holding deck/variant references, the user-editable card play order, per-card and global timer maps, and preset/LDR/usage metadata.

nodes.name: SessionStore.swift
nodes.summary: Brain of the card session flow — owns card navigation, records per-card results, and persists the completed CardSession, CardResults and DeckProgress to SwiftData, then enqueues a Supabase sync.
nodes.languageNotes: @Observable @MainActor store; ModelContext created fresh at write time, dependencies injected via init, OSLog logging.

nodes.name: SessionStore
nodes.summary: @Observable @MainActor store advancing through a deck's cards, computing progress and result tallies, and saving session/result/progress records plus enqueuing a SyncManager task on completion.

nodes.name: saveSession
nodes.summary: Writes the completed CardSession, its CardResults and DeckProgress to SwiftData, resolves the session number, and enqueues an async Supabase sync via SyncManager.

nodes.name: updateDeckProgress
nodes.summary: Updates or creates DeckProgress.currentCardIndex mid-session so the couple can resume from the correct card if they leave.

nodes.name: SessionView.swift
nodes.summary: Thin render-only card session view driven entirely by SessionStore — shows the current card, progress pips, skip/bookmark/discussed controls, and a session-complete summary.
nodes.languageNotes: Screenshot-protected; all logic delegated to the injected SessionStore.

nodes.name: SessionView
nodes.summary: View struct rendering session content (top bar, card text, pips, controls) or the completion summary based on SessionStore state, forwarding taps to recordAndAdvance.

nodes.name: SettingsView.swift
nodes.summary: Settings screen with profile, partner pairing, appearance, privacy, data export and danger-zone sections, plus DEBUG-only presence-debug and logout-reset entries.
nodes.languageNotes: NavigationStack root; AppStorage-backed screenshot-protection toggle; DEBUG-only dev rows.

nodes.name: SettingsView
nodes.summary: View struct composing the settings sections, pairing-code copy interaction, screenshot-protection toggle, and reset-all-data confirmation alert.

nodes.name: DebugLogoutView
nodes.summary: DEBUG-only button that signs out via AuthService and resets onboarding durable state via AppState so launch reconcile cannot flip onboarding back to complete.

nodes.name: deck-index.json
nodes.summary: Manifest listing the available bundled deck content ids — currently just 'the-opener'.

nodes.name: the-opener.json
nodes.summary: Authored card-deck content file for 'The Opener' deck — the ordered prompt cards a couple plays in a session.

nodes.name: Vayl.entitlements
nodes.summary: App capability entitlements enabling Sign in with Apple (Default) and complete file data protection (NSFileProtectionComplete).

nodes.name: Vayl.plist
nodes.summary: Info.plist supplying Supabase URL/anon-key build settings and registering the bundled Clash Display, Switzer, Zodiak, Volkhov and IBM Plex Mono fonts via UIAppFonts.

nodes.name: Vayl.storekit
nodes.summary: StoreKit test configuration defining the single non-consumable 'Vayl Core Lifetime' product (com.vayl.core.lifetime, $24.99) backing the paywall.

nodes.name: CandleIntensityTests.swift
nodes.summary: Unit tests asserting CandleIntensity's ordered cases match the experience-row slots and map to the correct NMStage values.

nodes.name: ContextOptionTests.swift
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the reason-based ContextPhase redesign — verifies cell option counts, solo single-anchoring, exploring/experienced set sharing, register derivation, and full RelationshipContext reachability.

nodes.name: GettingStartedTests.swift
nodes.summary: Unit tests for GettingStarted.resolve — checks step ordering and active/done/locked states across paired/unpaired and map-completion permutations.

nodes.name: MonteRowGeometryTests.swift
nodes.summary: Unit test verifying AppLayout.monteFanLayout produces an upright center card, mirror-symmetric outer angles/offsets, and a fan that fits within the screen width.
